name: Spin / Indoor Cycling (40-min)
description: 40-minute indoor cycling class. Two distinct peak waves separated by a recovery dip. Trance / progressive-house / EDM aesthetic. Tracks tend to be long (5-7 min) so each block of climb/sprint can ride a single extended remix.

music_pedagogy.beat: Cadence-locked. Climbs sit at 60-80 BPM (heavy resistance, slow turnover); sprints at 130-150 BPM. The track's BPM IS the rider's cadence — pick deliberately.
music_pedagogy.voice: Spin instructors cue cadence and resistance rapidly. Vocal-heavy tracks crowd the cue space. Long instrumental builds are valuable.
music_pedagogy.count: 8-count and 16-count are common for resistance changes. EDM build-and-drop structures map naturally to climbs (build) and sprints (drop).

phases.name: Warmup
phases.notes: Single 3-4 min track, mid energy, no surprises.

phases.name: Build
phases.notes: Progressive house build. Resistance is climbing.

phases.name: Peak Wave 1
phases.notes: First sprint / climb peak. Use long extended-mix trance or EDM anthems.

phases.name: Recovery
phases.notes: Brief comedown. Riders catch breath. ~3-5 min track.

phases.name: Peak Wave 2
phases.notes: Bigger peak than wave 1. Final sprint. Bring the anthem.

phases.name: Cool Down / Anthem
phases.notes: Balearic / chill-out / emotional closer. Often ends on a vocal anthem.

_inspiration: Structure derived from Tony Koop's Spin-1, Spin-2, Spin-3 public playlists (spotify.com/user/tonykoop). Track choices in those playlists are 2011-2012 era; the structural pattern is what's portable. New playlists should reach for current-era artists in the same lanes (prog house, trance, EDM).
